trivia_bullets.text: Bashful Beastie is from Duskmourn: House of Horror (set code DSK), released 2024-09-27 — a top-down horror set designed around a single sentient haunted house rather than a full plane, inspired by 1970s-1980s horror film aesthetics. The set's creative brief distinguished it from Innistrad by targeting modern pop-culture horror tropes rather than gothic literature. [Wizards of the Coast, Duskmourn Vision Design Handoff articles; Scryfall set data]

Flavor text: "No one knows what hides beneath the beasties' masks." The line positions the beastie's mask as a mystery within the house — consistent with Duskmourn's design philosophy of suspense through partial information. [Scryfall]

Bashful Beastie is a 5/4 common for {4}{G} whose death trigger activates manifest dread — a Duskmourn-exclusive evolution of the original Manifest mechanic from Fate Reforged (2015). Where original Manifest placed the top card of the library face-down blind, Manifest Dread lets you look at the top two and choose which to put on the battlefield face-down, sending the other to the graveyard. The graveyard-filling angle supports Duskmourn's delirium synergies. EDHREC records the card appearing in approximately 1,310 Commander decks as of 2024. [Scryfall; EDHREC; magic.wizards.com/en/news/feature/duskmourn-house-of-horror-mechanics; coolstuffinc.com/a/brandonashcraft-seo-11072024-the-mechanics-of-duskmourn-manifest-dread]

Art is by Aaron Miller, a freelance fantasy illustrator who has contributed to Magic since 2013 and is known within the community for a popular foil token series funded via Kickstarter and Patreon. Hipsters of the Coast covered two of his Kickstarter campaigns (2019, 2020) centered on original token art and custom sleeve collaborations. [Hipsters of the Coast; Gatherer artist search]
